title: Western blot for tissue extract
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"du7o7","text":"Protocol for the detection of proteins by Western blot from tissue extract ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start:
{"blocks":[{"key":"dji9j","text":"Perform protein extraction from snap-frozen brain tissue:

- weigh tissue
- add RIPA buffer (see Materials) : 10 X of the weight (40 mg = 400 µL)
- homogenize samples using sample homogenizer
- sonicate samples at 4 degrees C, 3 times 15 seconds (keep the samples on ice between each sonication)
- centrifuge samples at 6000 g for 10 minutes at 4 degrees C
- collect supernatant and measure protein concentration
- aliquot protein extracts and store at -20 degrees ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare sample for western blot 15 µg of protein in 12 µL, PBS Add 4 µL, Laemmli buffer Boil at 98 °C for 00:10:00
Load samples, together with 7 µL of mass marker (PageRuler‱ Plus Prestained Protein Ladder) on a on 4–15% Criterion‱ Tris-HCl Protein Gel.

Transfer the proteins on PVDF membrane (Trans-Blot‱ TurboTM PVDF Membrane) using Trans-Blot Turbo Transfer System (Bio-Rad), using the pre-programmed protocol STANDARD SD: 00:30:00 , up to 1.0 A, 25 V.
Block membranes for 01:00:00 using 5% milk dissolved in PBS-T 0,1% at Room temperature
Incubate membranes Overnight in primary antibody in 5% milk PBS-T 0,1% at 4 °C
Wash membranes with PBS-T 0,1% for 10 minutes, 3 times at T Room temperature Wash PBS-T 00:10:00 Wash PBS-T 00:10:00 Wash PBS-T 00:10:00
Incubate membranes for 01:00:00 horseradish peroxidase-conjugated secondaryantibody (Dako) diluted 1/10000 in 5% milk PBS-T 0,1% at Room temperature
Wash membranes with PBS-T 0,1% for 10 minutes, 3 times at T Room temperature Wash PBS-T 00:10:00 Wash PBS-T 00:10:00 Wash PBS-T 00:10:00
Develop membranes using ECL Prime (GE Healthcare)
creator: María Sanchiz Calvo
